danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct character models matching film antagonists (Nazis, cultists, skeletal bosses)
Pits, falling platforms, moving obstacles in side-scrolling view
danger_signals.meaning: Platforming challenges that cause death/restart at checkpoint

danger_signals.visual_form: Walter Donovan skeleton sprite, larger enemy variants
danger_signals.meaning: Climactic confrontations requiring sustained combat or pattern learning

danger_signals.visual_form: Rapid enemy attack patterns with minimal safe zones
danger_signals.meaning: High difficulty spike; damage is nearly inevitable without precise timing

safety_signals.visual_form: Level progression points (implicit in restart mechanics)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression points; death restarts from checkpoint rather than level start

safety_signals.visual_form: Gun sprites with unlimited ammo, grenade pickups
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced combat capability; temporary power boost against threats
Safe ranged attack method; also used for traversal (swinging across pits)

faction_indicators.faction: Nazi forces (Raiders of the Lost Ark)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Implied through enemy sprite design matching film antagonists
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Desert/temple settings associated with Nazi archaeology quests

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Implied through enemy sprite design (cultist garb, ritualistic context)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Underground temple settings, mine cart sequences

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Implied through boss design (Walter Donovan skeleton)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Raiders of the Lost Ark narrative (beginning state)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Desert temples, Nazi guards, artifact-focused environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard platforming with whip-based puzzle solving; enemy AI patterns match Nazi soldier behavior

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Temple of Doom narrative (unlocked via progression)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Underground caverns, mine carts, ritualistic architecture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Introduces vehicle gameplay (mine cart, raft); cultist enemy patterns differ from Nazi soldiers

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Last Crusade narrative (unlocked via progression or password)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle interiors, crusade-era stonework, grail chamber aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounters intensify; Walter Donovan skeletal form indicates grail corruption state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sprite density increases; enemy placement becomes more chaotic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies cluster with unavoidable hit patterns; platform gaps narrow; Mode 7 effects may mask safe zones

environmental_storytelling.example: Desert temple designs, mine cart sequences, castle architecture visually reference corresponding film locations
Nazi guards signal Raiders section; cultists signal Temple of Doom; skeletal bosses signal grail curse progression
Story conveyed through cutscenes between levels and text explanations; mostly faithful to three-film narrative
Vehicle sequences (plane, mine cart, raft) interrupt standard platforming to reflect film spectacle moments

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Side-scrolling movement; whip attacks as primary offense; can punch or roll into enemies; swings whip for traversal
Attack on proximity; form clusters that create unavoidable hit scenarios; vary by faction (Nazi vs. cultist patterns)
Large sprite variant; requires sustained combat or pattern-based avoidance; signals narrative climax
Gaps narrow, moving obstacles accelerate, pit complexity increases; Mode 7 effects visually obscure safe zones

notes: Game uses Super Star Wars engine with Mode 7 visual effects to enhance sprite-based presentation. Three-movie structure (Raiders, Temple of Doom, Last Crusade) creates three distinct visual/behavioral zones. Difficulty spike and 'unavoidable hits' mechanic creates high friction between safe state (checkpoint) and danger state (enemy encounter). Whip duality (weapon + tool) is core to danger/safety communication—it enables both offense and traversal. Visual design praised for faithfulness to films despite technical SNES limitations. Narrative progression gates later films (Temple, Crusade) behind level completion or password entry, creating gating metaphor in game structure.
